title: Review: daemon/cli/tui/bot round 19
notes: Confidence calibration (Section 7): ~80% confident no additional P0 bugs in the reviewed scope. Highest residual uncertainty is in jmap.rs (2700 lines) around JMAP method dispatch edge cases and ULID/UUID ID aliasing between JMAP object IDs and relay message IDs — these were reviewed but the code is dense. The daemon/relay.rs peer-delivered chat_id injection (nie-f0pz.1) is the highest-severity confirmed finding. Maintainer risk: MEDIUM — jmap.rs is large and has several patterns (inline error construction, two-query paths) that will accumulate technical debt; the core security surface (auth, MLS, HPKE) is well-structured.
